question: Total water withdrawal by source (ML): surface, groundwater, seawater, produced, third-party
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Separate freshwater (≤1,000 mg/L TDS) from other water. Identify withdrawals from water-stressed areas.

question: Total water discharge by destination type (ML)
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Break down freshwater vs. other water. Report discharge to water-stressed areas separately.

question: Total water consumption (ML): all areas and water-stressed areas separately
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Calculate as: withdrawal minus discharge. Report change in water storage if relevant.

question: Inputs, activities, and outputs leading to significant waste impacts
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Map the waste generation hotspots in the organization's own operations and value chain.

question: Actions to prevent waste generation and manage waste third-party compliance
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Describe circularity measures, waste reduction targets, and auditing of waste contractors.

question: Total waste generated (metric tons) by composition
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Break down by waste stream type (plastic, organic, electronic, construction, etc.).

question: Total waste diverted from disposal: hazardous and non-hazardous (metric tons)
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Break down by reuse, recycling, and other recovery. Separate onsite vs. offsite recovery.

question: Total waste directed to disposal: hazardous and non-hazardous (metric tons)
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Break down by incineration (with/without energy recovery), landfill, other. Separate onsite vs. offsite.

question: Total materials used (weight/volume): renewable and non-renewable
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Report in metric tons or liters. Distinguish raw materials, packaging, and processing materials.

question: % of recycled input materials used
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Formula: (Recycled input materials ÷ Total input materials) × 100. Specify material types included.

question: % of reclaimed products and packaging by product category
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Describe take-back schemes, EPR programs. State how reclaim data is collected.

question: Policies to halt and reverse biodiversity loss aligned with Kunming-Montreal GBF
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Reference 2030 Targets and 2050 Goals from the Global Biodiversity Framework. Link to public commitments.

question: Goals and targets to halt biodiversity loss; base year and indicators
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: State if targets are science-based (e.g., SBTN Nature). List monitoring indicators.

question: How organization determined sites and supply chain with most significant biodiversity impacts
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Describe screening methodology (proximity to protected areas, threatened species presence, etc.).

question: Location and size (hectares) of sites with most significant biodiversity impacts
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: For each site: state proximity to sensitive areas, ecosystem type, and activities conducted.

question: Products/services in supply chain with significant biodiversity impacts
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Identify commodities (palm oil, soy, timber, etc.) and countries/jurisdictions of origin.

question: Operational sites in or adjacent to protected areas: location, type, size, biodiversity status
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: For each site: GPS/map reference, size (km2), operation type, IUCN/Ramsar/national listing.

question: Land/sea use change, resource exploitation, pollution, invasive species by site
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Report ecosystem converted (ha, type before/after), species harvested, pollutants, invasive species risk.

question: Significant direct and indirect impacts on biodiversity
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Describe species affected, area impacted, duration, and reversibility of each significant impact.

question: Changes to ecosystem type, size, and condition since base year
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Report per site: ecosystem type, size (ha), condition score for base year and current period.

question: Ecosystem services and beneficiaries affected by activities
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: List ecosystem services (water purification, pollination, carbon storage, etc.) and affected stakeholders.

question: Mitigation hierarchy applied: avoid, minimize, restore, offset, transform
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: For each site: describe specific actions per mitigation hierarchy level. Report restoration area (ha).

question: Habitats protected or restored: size, location, status, partnerships
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Report in km2 or ha. State if success verified by independent professionals. List third-party partnerships.

question: IUCN Red List species in areas affected by operations
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Provide count by extinction risk category: Critically Endangered, Endangered, Vulnerable, Near Threatened, Least Concern.

question: Process for ABS regulatory compliance and voluntary benefit-sharing actions
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Reference Nagoya Protocol obligations. Describe how benefits are shared with communities and countries of origin.

question: Total monetary value of political contributions by country and recipient
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Include direct and indirect contributions. Describe methodology for valuing in-kind contributions.

question: % of product/service categories assessed for health and safety impacts
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: State whether assessment was by organization or third party. List assessment methodology.

question: Incidents of non-compliance on product/service H&S (fines, warnings, voluntary code breaches)
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Break down by: regulatory fines, regulatory warnings, voluntary code violations.

question: Product/service labeling requirements: sourcing, content, safe use, disposal info
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: State % of significant product categories covered by labeling assessment procedures.

question: Incidents of non-compliance on product labeling
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Same breakdown as GRI 416-2. State if no incidents were identified.

question: Incidents of non-compliance in marketing communications
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Same breakdown as GRI 416-2. Covers advertising, promotion, and sponsorship.

question: Substantiated complaints on customer privacy breaches and data losses
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Break down by external complaints and regulatory body complaints. Include identified data leaks/thefts.

question: GRI Content Index (mandatory): all disclosures, page refs, omissions with reasons
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Must be published. List every GRI disclosure reported, with page/URL reference and reason for any omission.

question: External assurance provider name, qualifications, and independence
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Name the assurance firm, lead auditor qualifications, and confirm independence from the organization.

question: Scope of assurance, standard used, and assurance level
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: State standard (ISAE 3000, AA1000AS v3) and level (limited or reasonable). List disclosures covered.

question: Data quality management and internal controls for sustainability data
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: Describe data collection processes, review gates, and any verification before publication.

question: Significant limitations or exclusions in the sustainability report scope
sector: Technology, Manufacturing, Finance, Retail, Energy, Healthcare, Transportation, Construction, Agriculture
guidelines: State any topics, entities, or geographies excluded and reasons.

question: • The organization's position in the oil and gas value chain (upstream, midstream, downstream, or combination)
guidelines: Describe whether the organisation operates upstream (exploration/production), midstream (transport/storage), downstream (refining/retail), or across multiple segments; reference OGMP 2.0 or equivalent sector classification.

question: • Type(s) of reserves or resources: conventional vs unconventional, proven and probable reserves
guidelines: Report proved and probable reserves (1P and 2P) by type (conventional oil, natural gas, unconventional) in million barrels of oil equivalent (mmboe), per SEC or SPE-PRMS definitions.

question: • Absolute Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions (metric tonnes CO2 equivalent)
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Absolute Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions (metric tonnes CO2 equivalent); follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Methane emissions (absolute and intensity: kg CH4 per unit of production)
guidelines: Report gross methane emissions in metric tons CH₄ and CO₂e; calculate intensity per unit of production (kg CH₄/boe); use OGMP 2.0 Level 4 or Level 5 measurement methods.

question: • Flaring volumes (gross and routine flaring, in m3 or tonnes)
guidelines: Report routine flaring and total flaring volumes in m³ or metric tons separately; state progress against zero routine flaring commitments (World Bank initiative).

question: • Plans and targets for reducing GHG emissions, including methane and flaring reduction targets
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Plans and targets for reducing GHG emissions, including methane and flaring redu; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Participation in carbon trading or offsetting mechanisms
guidelines: List participation in compliance carbon markets (EU ETS, California cap-and-trade) and voluntary offset programs; report total credits purchased/retired in tCO₂e and verification standard (Gold Standard, Verra).

question: • Physical climate risks and transition risks that may affect the organization's assets, operations, or business model
guidelines: List asset classes or specific facilities at risk from physical climate events; disclose the value of assets at risk and adaptation investments planned.

question: • Opportunities related to low-carbon products, renewable energy, or carbon capture
guidelines: Identify specific commercial opportunities (e.g., hydrogen, CCS, renewables JVs) with estimated revenue potential and planned investment; reference alignment with IEA NZE scenario.

question: • Scenario analysis used, including 1.5°C-aligned scenarios
guidelines: State the specific scenarios modelled (IEA NZE, IEA SDS, IPCC 1.5°C), the modelling tool used, the key variables tested (carbon price, demand shifts, stranded assets), and the financial sensitivities produced.

question: • Capital expenditure plans for low-carbon energy transitions
guidelines: Disclose the total capex allocated to low-carbon or clean energy projects (renewable energy, CCS, hydrogen, biofuels) as a value and as % of total capex; include a 5-year forward outlook.

question: Air Quality
guidelines: This section covers air quality disclosures for the sector. Report all significant air pollutants (NOx, SOx, VOCs, PM10, PM2.5, HAPs) in metric tons per year; describe emission measurement methodology, abatement technologies deployed, and compliance status with applicable air quality regulations.

question: • Significant air pollutants beyond GHGs: NOx, SOx, VOCs, particulates, HAPs
guidelines: Report each pollutant in metric tons per year, measured at the point of emission; reference the emission measurement methodology and applicable regulatory limits for each jurisdiction.

question: • Emission reduction measures and technology used
guidelines: Describe specific abatement technologies deployed (electrification of drives, closed-loop flaring, catalytic reduction); state the emission reduction achieved (tCO₂e or tonnes pollutant) and the investment made.

question: • Incidents of non-compliance with air emission regulations
guidelines: Report the total number of exceedances of permitted air emission limits, the pollutant involved, the magnitude and duration of exceedance, and the corrective actions taken.

question: Water & Waste
guidelines: This section covers water and waste management disclosures. Report total water withdrawal and consumption by source, water recycling rates, total waste generated by type, hazardous waste disposal methods, and any incidents of water or waste non-compliance.

question: • Total water withdrawn and consumed, by source, in water-stressed areas
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Total water withdrawn and consumed, by source, in water-stressed areas; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Volume and quality of produced water generated and managed
guidelines: Report total produced water volume (barrels or m³), water quality parameters (TDS, pH, oil content), and disposition method (reinjection %, recycled %, treated and discharged %); target zero discharge where feasible.

question: • Incidents of water pollution or non-compliance
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Incidents of water pollution or non-compliance; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Water recycling and reuse rates
guidelines: Calculate water recycling rate as: (Volume reused/recycled ÷ Total water withdrawal) × 100; describe specific recycling technologies employed and targets for improvement.

question: • Total hazardous and non-hazardous waste generated
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Total hazardous and non-hazardous waste generated; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Waste disposal methods used; drilling waste management
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Waste disposal methods used; drilling waste management; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Radioactive waste (NORM) generated and managed
guidelines: Report the volume (cubic metres) and activity level of Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material (NORM) waste; describe licensed disposal routes and regulatory compliance status.

question: Decommissioning
guidelines: This section covers decommissioning obligations. Disclose the number of assets at various decommissioning stages, total financial provision made for decommissioning liabilities (plugging, abandonment, site restoration), and the methodology used to estimate future costs.

question: • Number of active, closed, and abandoned well sites
guidelines: Report the number of wells by status (producing, shut-in, temporarily abandoned, permanently plugged and abandoned); include wells awaiting plug and abandon operations.

question: • Financial provisions for plugging and abandonment liabilities
guidelines: Disclose the total financial provision (in reporting currency) made for future plugging, abandonment, and site restoration liabilities; describe the provisioning methodology and regulatory basis.

question: • Decommissioning plans for platforms, pipelines, and refineries
guidelines: Describe the decommissioning schedule for end-of-life assets, the regulatory approval status, the estimated cost, and the environmental management plan for each major asset.

question: • Land remediation area and status
guidelines: Report the total area (hectares) requiring land remediation, the area remediated during the year, and remediation status (active, completed, planned) with site-specific risk assessments.

question: Spills
guidelines: This section covers spills and releases. Report all significant spills by number, volume (barrels or metric tons), material type, and environmental impact; describe containment response, regulatory notifications made, and remediation outcomes.

question: • Number and type of significant spills, leaks, and releases
guidelines: Report each significant spill (above regulatory reporting threshold) by type (oil, chemicals, produced water), volume, and location; include offshore and onshore incidents separately.

question: • Volume, nature, and type of material spilled
guidelines: For each reportable spill, disclose the volume in barrels or metric tons, the material (crude oil, refined product, chemical, produced water), and whether it reached a sensitive receptor.

question: • Impacts of spills and remediation efforts
guidelines: Describe the ecological and community impacts of each significant spill; report the area affected (m² or hectares), remediation actions taken, cost of remediation, and regulatory findings.

question: Process Safety
guidelines: This section covers process safety performance. Report Tier 1 and Tier 2 process safety events per API RP 754 or equivalent; disclose fatalities, TRIR, and LTIFR for employees and contractors; describe the process safety management system framework applied.

question: • Process safety events (Tier 1 and Tier 2 by API RP 754)
guidelines: Report separately the number of Tier 1 (most serious) and Tier 2 process safety events per API RP 754 definitions; include the PSIR (Process Safety Incident Rate) per 200,000 hours worked.

question: • Work-related fatalities, high-consequence injuries, recordable injuries
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Work-related fatalities, high-consequence injuries, recordable injuries; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • TRIR, LTIR, and fatality rate for employees and contractors
guidelines: Calculate TRIR as: (Recordable injuries × 200,000) ÷ Hours worked; LTIFR as: (Lost-time injuries × 1,000,000) ÷ Hours worked; report separately for employees and contractors.

question: • Approach to process safety and major hazard management
guidelines: Describe the process safety management (PSM) framework used (e.g., OSHA PSM, API RP 750), bow-tie risk assessments, Layer of Protection Analysis (LOPA), and Management of Change procedures.

question: • Health monitoring for occupational exposures
guidelines: Describe biological monitoring and health surveillance programs for workers exposed to chemical hazards (benzene, H₂S, silica, asbestos); report the number of workers monitored and any occupational illness cases.

question: Social & Community
guidelines: This section covers community and social impact disclosures. Report local hiring percentages, community investment amounts, grievances received and resolved, resettlement programs, payments to governments by country, and FPIC processes for indigenous-affected operations.

question: • Local hiring percentages at significant locations
guidelines: Calculate % of workforce (employees + contractors) who are nationals or residents of the local community, at each significant operational location; define 'local' explicitly.

question: • Non-discrimination and equal pay policies and performance
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Non-discrimination and equal pay policies and performance; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Policies on freedom of association and collective bargaining in the supply chain
guidelines: Identify the supply chain nodes most exposed to climate risk; describe assessments conducted and mitigation actions planned or underway.

question: • Incidents of discrimination and corrective actions taken
guidelines: Describe each corrective action taken in response to significant complaints, including timelines and outcomes.

question: • Pay equity analysis by gender and minority status
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Pay equity analysis by gender and minority status; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Operations and suppliers at risk of forced labor or child labor
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Operations and suppliers at risk of forced labor or child labor; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Actions to eliminate forced labor and child labor from the value chain
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Actions to eliminate forced labor and child labor from the value chain; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Significant economic impacts, positive and negative, on local communities
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Significant economic impacts, positive and negative, on local communities; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Local procurement percentages
guidelines: Calculate % of goods and services procurement spend sourced from locally owned businesses in the communities surrounding each operation; define 'local' and state the target.

question: • Payments to governments (taxes, royalties, bonuses, dividends) — country-by-country
guidelines: Complete country-by-country reporting disclosing all payments to government entities (production taxes, royalties, signature bonuses, pipeline fees, dividends to state-owned entities) per EITI or GRI 207-4 requirements.

question: • Operations with significant actual and potential impacts on local communities
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Operations with significant actual and potential impacts on local communities; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Community development programs and investments
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Community development programs and investments; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Community grievance mechanisms and resolution statistics
guidelines: Report the total number of community grievances received, categorised by issue type; state the number resolved within the agreed timeframe, average resolution time, and the number currently pending.

question: • Processes for identifying and addressing potential impacts on communities' land and resource rights
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Processes for identifying and addressing potential impacts on communities' land ; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Compensation paid for land use
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Compensation paid for land use; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Incidents of land rights violations
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Incidents of land rights violations; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Operations in areas with a presence of Indigenous Peoples
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Operations in areas with a presence of Indigenous Peoples; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Free, Prior and Informed Consent (FPIC) processes
guidelines: Describe the FPIC process applied to each project affecting Indigenous Peoples: initial consultation, documentation of consent or objection, outcomes, and any ongoing dispute resolution.

question: • Incidents of violations of rights of Indigenous Peoples and outcomes
guidelines: Report the number of confirmed incidents (e.g., unauthorised access to sacred sites, cultural heritage damage), their status (under review, resolved), and remediation outcomes.

question: • Operations in conflict-affected and high-risk areas
guidelines: Identify operations in or near OECD-defined conflict-affected areas; describe the enhanced due diligence process applied under OECD Guidance, security arrangements, and human rights impact assessments.

question: • Use of private security forces — vetting, training on human rights
guidelines: State whether a standalone Human Rights Policy exists, referencing the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights; describe its scope and approval level.

question: • Reported incidents involving security forces and responses
guidelines: Report each incident involving security personnel (excessive use of force, injury, community complaint) and describe the investigation process, corrective actions, and outcomes.

question: Governance & Compliance
guidelines: This section covers governance and compliance disclosures. Report confirmed corruption incidents and outcomes, anti-competitive conduct cases, internal audit findings on compliance, and the anti-bribery training completion rate for employees and third parties.

question: • Legal actions pending or completed regarding anti-competitive behavior
guidelines: Report all pending and resolved antitrust/competition law cases; describe the nature of each allegation, jurisdiction, current status, and financial exposure or settlement amount.

question: • Total number and nature of confirmed incidents of corruption
guidelines: Report total confirmed corruption incidents involving employees or third parties, categorised by type (bribery, fraud, embezzlement), and state the investigation and disciplinary outcomes for each.

question: • Anti-corruption training and policies
guidelines: State whether the anti-bribery and corruption policy is aligned with the UN Convention Against Corruption and local laws; confirm CEO-level endorsement.

question: • Confirmed incidents involving public officials
guidelines: Separately disclose incidents involving public officials or government entities; describe the nature, outcome (prosecution, settlement, contract termination), and systemic prevention measures taken.

question: • Country-by-country reporting: revenues, taxes paid, royalties, license fees, bonuses, dividends
guidelines: Quantify the potential revenue impact of each material climate risk/opportunity (range in USD/local currency); describe the assumptions and methodology used.

question: • Production entitlements, profit oil, dividends from state-owned enterprise interests
guidelines: Disclose volumes and values of production entitlements, profit oil/gas shares, and dividends paid to or received from state-owned enterprises, per EITI or GRI 207-4 country-by-country requirements.

question: General
guidelines: This section covers general sector-specific disclosures. Provide the fundamental sector context including production volumes by type, operational footprint (number and type of facilities), countries of operation, and the material topics identified for each jurisdiction.

question: • Whether the organization mines thermal coal, metallurgical coal, or both
guidelines: State clearly whether the organisation mines thermal coal (for power generation), metallurgical/coking coal (for steel production), or both; provide production volumes by type in metric tons.

question: • Total coal production (tonnes) by type
guidelines: Report total run-of-mine (ROM) and marketable coal production separately for thermal and metallurgical coal; include year-on-year change and production from owned vs. managed mines.

question: • Number and type of mines (surface/open-cast, underground, in-situ gasification)
guidelines: List each operational mine by name, type (surface open-cut, underground longwall, room-and-pillar, in-situ), location (country, region), and current production status.

question: • Countries of operation and material topics explained with reasons for each
guidelines: List each country of operation; for each, identify the material ESG topics (water scarcity, community displacement, land access, regulatory compliance) that are most significant given local context.

question: Emissions & Climate
guidelines: This section covers GHG and climate disclosures for the sector. Report absolute Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions in tCO₂e; disclose methane intensity, flaring volumes, climate scenario analysis results, GHG reduction targets, and capital allocation for low-carbon transition.

question: • Absolute Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions (tonnes CO2e)
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Absolute Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions (tonnes CO2e); follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Methane liberated from coal seams (cubic meters and CO2e)
guidelines: Report methane liberated during coal mining in m³ and CO₂e; break down into captured (for energy or flared) vs. ventilation air methane; compare to IPCC Tier 2 or site-specific emission factors.

question: • Methane captured and utilized (m3)
guidelines: Report volume of methane captured (m³) and its utilisation (power generation, sold to grid, flared); disclose avoided emissions achieved by methane capture vs. venting.

question: • Alignment with national or international coal phase-out timelines
guidelines: State whether the organisation has publicly committed to a coal phase-out date; reference any applicable national energy policy targets (e.g., national NDC) or investor expectations from climate-active shareholders.

question: • Plans and targets for reducing GHG emissions
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Plans and targets for reducing GHG emissions; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Stranded asset risk assessment
guidelines: Describe the methodology used to assess stranded asset risk (discounted cash flow under carbon price scenarios, IEA demand scenarios); disclose the proportion of assets that may be uneconomic under a 1.5°C pathway.

question: • Transition risks under different carbon price scenarios
guidelines: Model financial impact (EBITDA, NPV of reserves) under at least two carbon price trajectories (e.g., USD 50/t vs USD 150/t CO₂e); state the assumptions and time horizon.

question: • Diversification strategies
guidelines: Describe strategic investments in non-coal activities (renewables, battery minerals, land rehabilitation services, skill redeployment programs); report capital allocated to diversification as % of total capex.

question: Air Quality
guidelines: This section covers air quality disclosures for the sector. Report all significant air pollutants (NOx, SOx, VOCs, PM10, PM2.5, HAPs) in metric tons per year; describe emission measurement methodology, abatement technologies deployed, and compliance status with applicable air quality regulations.

question: • Significant air pollutants: dust/particulates, NOx, SOx from mining and processing
guidelines: Report PM10 and PM2.5 dust emissions, NOx and SOx from blasting and diesel equipment; describe dust suppression programs and compliance with applicable ambient air quality standards.

question: • Dust management plans; spontaneous combustion management
guidelines: Describe dust management plans (road watering, enclosures, windbreaks) and coal spontaneous combustion monitoring and management protocols (temperature monitoring, gas monitoring, inertisation).

question: Land & Biodiversity
guidelines: This section covers land disturbance and biodiversity disclosures. Report total area disturbed vs. rehabilitated (hectares), operations in or adjacent to protected areas, biodiversity offsets committed, and the mitigation hierarchy applied.

question: • Land disturbed vs. rehabilitated (hectares)
guidelines: Report total land area cumulatively disturbed (hectares) and total area rehabilitated and relinquished during the reporting period; track against the approved Land Rehabilitation Plan.

question: • Operations in or adjacent to protected areas; biodiversity offsets
guidelines: List all operations within 10 km of IUCN Category I–VI areas, Ramsar sites, or UNESCO World Heritage Sites; describe biodiversity offsets committed and the mitigation hierarchy applied.

question: Water
guidelines: This section covers water management disclosures specific to the sector. Report total water withdrawal and consumption by source, produced water or process water volumes, water recycling rates, AMD or effluent management practices, and regulatory compliance status.

question: • Total water withdrawn and consumed by source
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Total water withdrawn and consumed by source; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Acid mine drainage (AMD) volumes generated, treated, and released
guidelines: Report volumes of AMD generated (m³/day), treatment capacity and method (neutralisation, passive treatment), treatment performance (pH, sulphate, heavy metals), and discharge compliance status.

question: • Mine water balance
guidelines: Present a water balance for each mine site showing sources (rainfall, groundwater ingress, recycled water) vs. consumption (dust suppression, processing, evaporation) and discharge.

question: • Compliance with effluent discharge limits
guidelines: Report the number and nature of exceedances of permitted effluent discharge limits; describe corrective actions and any penalties imposed by environmental regulators.

question: Tailings & Waste
guidelines: This section covers tailings and mining waste disclosures. Report all TSFs by name, location, capacity, consequence classification, and safety review outcomes; disclose waste rock volumes, AMD potential, and any TSF failures or near-misses.

question: • Number, location, and size of tailings storage facilities (TSF)
guidelines: Provide a register of all TSFs with location, storage capacity (m³ or tonnes), current volume stored, construction method (upstream, centreline, downstream), and dam safety classification.

question: • Classification of TSFs by consequence category
guidelines: Classify each TSF per the MAC Tailings Guide or ICOLD consequence classification (Extreme, Very High, High, Significant); describe the basis for classification.

question: • Independent tailings review results
guidelines: Report the date, scope, and key findings of the most recent Independent Tailings Review Board (ITRB) review; disclose any critical recommendations and the implementation status.

question: • Emergency preparedness plans for TSF failure
guidelines: Describe the Emergency Preparedness and Response Plan (EPRP) for each TSF, including population at risk, inundation modelling, early warning system, and evacuation procedures.

question: • Number of TSF failures or near-misses
guidelines: Report any TSF failures, partial failures, or near-miss events during the period; describe the cause, environmental/community impact, and remediation actions taken.

question: • Mine waste rock volumes generated and managed
guidelines: Report total waste rock generated (metric tons or m³), the acid rock drainage potential classification (PAF, NAF), and the engineered management method for each waste rock category.

question: • Spontaneous combustion incidents in waste dumps
guidelines: Report the number of spontaneous combustion events in waste dumps; describe the monitoring system (temperature sensors, gas monitoring) and response procedures employed.

question: • Rehabilitation of overburden dumps
guidelines: Report the total area of overburden dumps rehabilitated (hectares) during the year, the revegetation success criteria, and compliance with the approved rehabilitation program.

question: Mine Closure
guidelines: This section covers mine closure disclosures. Report financial provisions for mine closure per site, approved closure plan status, post-closure land use commitments, and ARD long-term management obligations for each operation.

question: • Financial provisions for mine closure
guidelines: Disclose the total financial assurance (bond, trust fund, parent guarantee) provided to regulators for mine closure; describe the provisioning methodology and whether it is independently audited.

question: • Mine closure plans and timeline
guidelines: Describe the approved mine closure plan for each operation, including expected closure date, key closure activities, post-closure land use vision, and social transition measures.

question: • Post-closure land use commitments
guidelines: State the agreed post-closure land use for each mine site (agriculture, conservation, commercial use) and any binding commitments made to regulators or communities.

question: • Acid Rock Drainage (ARD) post-closure management
guidelines: Describe the ARD management commitments for the post-closure period (treatment plant operation, monitoring requirements) and the financial provision established for perpetual care.

question: Process Safety & OHS
guidelines: This section covers process safety and occupational health for mining. Report major incident statistics (collapses, inundations, explosions), TRIR and fatality rates for employees and contractors, occupational disease monitoring results (silicosis, NIHL), and emergency rescue capability.

question: • Significant incidents: mine collapses, inundations, explosions
guidelines: Report all major underground incidents (collapse, inundation, fire, explosion) with severity (injuries, fatalities), root cause analysis findings, and corrective actions implemented.

question: • Roof fall and rib fall incidents; seismicity management
guidelines: Report total roof/rib fall incidents by severity; describe the seismicity management plan, seismic monitoring network, exclusion zone protocols, and re-entry procedures.

question: • Fatalities, high-consequence injuries, TRIR, LTIFR
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Fatalities, high-consequence injuries, TRIR, LTIFR; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Coal dust exposure and dust-related disease monitoring (CWP/black lung rates)
guidelines: Report the number of workers enrolled in dust health surveillance programs; disclose incidence of Coal Workers' Pneumoconiosis (CWP/black lung) and corrective actions to reduce exposure below regulatory limits.

question: • Noise-induced hearing loss monitoring
guidelines: Report the number of workers monitored for NIHL, the number of audiometric threshold shift cases detected, and the noise control measures implemented (engineering controls, hearing protection).

question: • Silica exposure management
guidelines: Report the number of workers exposed to respirable crystalline silica above action levels; describe the engineering controls, respiratory protection program, and health surveillance outcomes.

question: • Emergency response drills and rescue teams
guidelines: Report the number and type of emergency response drills conducted; confirm the number of certified mine rescue team members and the rescue equipment maintained at each operation.

question: Social & Just Transition
guidelines: This section covers social responsibility and just transition disclosures. Report local employment rates, reskilling and transition programs for affected workers, pay gap analysis, CSR/community investment, resettlement statistics, FPIC implementation, and government payments.

question: • Local employment rates at operations
guidelines: Report the % of direct employees (and separately contract workforce) who are residents of local communities within a defined radius of the operation.

question: • Reskilling and just transition programs for workers
guidelines: Describe programs providing retraining, alternative employment, or financial support to workers whose jobs are at risk from mine closure or automation; report the number of workers enrolled.

question: • Number of redundancies planned related to mine closure
guidelines: Disclose the number of planned redundancies at each mine approaching closure, the timeline for redundancies, and the severance and transition support package offered.

question: • Pay gap analysis; discrimination incidents
guidelines: Report mean and median pay gaps by gender and, where data is available, by ethnicity; describe the methodology and corrective actions taken to address identified gaps.

question: • Assessment of forced labor risk in artisanal and small-scale mining
guidelines: Describe the due diligence process used to identify forced labour risks in ASM supply chains (e.g., supply chain mapping, third-party audits) and any corrective actions taken.

question: • Child labor prevention measures
guidelines: State the minimum working age policy and how it is enforced; describe age verification procedures in hiring, community awareness programs, and any incidents identified.

question: • Operations restricting freedom of association; collective bargaining coverage rates
guidelines: Report the % of workers covered by collective bargaining agreements; identify any operations where national law restricts freedom of association and describe compensating measures.

question: • Payments to governments (taxes, royalties, levies)
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Payments to governments (taxes, royalties, levies); follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Local procurement percentages
guidelines: Calculate % of goods and services procurement spend sourced from locally owned businesses in the communities surrounding each operation; define 'local' and state the target.

question: • Community impacts from noise, dust, blasting, water
guidelines: Describe monitoring programs for noise, dust, blasting vibration, and water quality at community receptor points; report on complaints received and regulatory compliance.

question: • Community development investments
guidelines: Report total community investment (amount, % of net profit) broken down by investment category (education, health, infrastructure, economic development) and the beneficiary reach.

question: • Number of community grievances and resolution rates
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: Number of community grievances and resolution rates; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Resettlement: number of households, compensation, livelihood restoration
guidelines: Report the number of households resettled during the year, the compensation standard applied (replacement cost), livelihood restoration measures, and the grievance outcome for resettled persons.

question: • Subsidence impacts and remediation
guidelines: Report the total area affected by ground subsidence above underground mines (hectares), the number of structures impacted, compensation paid, and remediation works completed.

question: • Land access agreements
guidelines: Describe the legal basis for land access at each operation (freehold, long-term lease, mining licence, surface access agreement); report any disputes or court proceedings regarding land access.

question: • FPIC processes; sacred site protection
guidelines: Report this sector-specific GRI disclosure: FPIC processes; sacred site protection; follow the GRI Oil & Gas or Mining sector supplement methodology and use industry-standard reporting protocols (API, IPIECA, ICMM as applicable).

question: • Security incidents at operations in high-risk areas; security provider vetting
guidelines: Report incidents involving security personnel at each high-risk location; describe the vetting and training standards applied to security contractors, including VPSHR compliance.

question: Governance
guidelines: This section covers sector-specific governance disclosures. Report confirmed corruption incidents and disciplinary outcomes, anti-bribery training completion rates, legal proceedings for anti-competitive conduct, and the governance oversight structure for ethics and compliance.

question: • Confirmed corruption incidents
guidelines: Report confirmed cases of bribery, facilitation payments, or other corruption involving employees or contractors; state disciplinary outcomes and systemic prevention measures taken.

question: • Anti-bribery training completion rates
guidelines: Report the % of employees and high-risk third parties (agents, brokers, customs brokers) who completed anti-bribery training during the year; distinguish first-time vs. refresher training.

question: Country-by-country: production, revenue, taxes paid, royalties, license fees
guidelines: Quantify the potential revenue impact of each material climate risk/opportunity (range in USD/local currency); describe the assumptions and methodology used.
